Reference

Glossary

The public term map for Plectis: every active governed vocabulary row is projected here as an expandable reader card, with preferred wording, search handles, examples, non-examples, scope boundaries, relationships, and source refs kept separate. These rows are generated from the governed system vocabulary registry.

Legacy name note. Microcosm is not a current glossary term for Plectis. It appears only in lineage, legacy URLs, compatibility identifiers, old source paths, and migration records. For the rename and old-route behavior, read Lineage & legacy and the legacy route manifest.
plectisPlectispublic_projectionPlectis is the current public display name for the source-linked AI-native workflow map.

public woven map Plectis is the human-facing public name for the map, public site, reader packets, and canonical public repository over the source-linked workflow system. Older technical compatibility surfaces still carry Microcosm identifiers such as microcosm-substrate/, .microcosm/, package names, schemas, and old URLs.

ScopeUse Plectis for current public-facing copy, public site routes, reader packets, and the canonical public source repository.

ExampleThe landing page, reader packets, and glossary should say Plectis when they mean the current public identity.

Not thisDo not use Plectis as proof that package ids, old URLs, .microcosm/ state, or historical source identifiers have all been renamed.

Reader ruleUse Plectis for active public-facing copy, glossary, landing-page brand, public repository references, and reader handoff surfaces. Keep Microcosm only for lineage, historical references, technical identifiers, compatibility URLs, lower-case schema/package ids, and old public source links.

Also shown asPlectis public map

Search handlesplectis source map, Latin plectere, plectere

Related termssupersedes_public_labelmicrocosmPlectis is the current human-facing public label; Microcosm remains a historical and technical compatibility label.usesstandardThe Plectis name is governed through the existing Plectis/Microcosm standard while compatibility identifiers remain supported.protectscold_agentCold readers should see Plectis as the public name while still resolving Microcosm compatibility identifiers.requiresliving_system_posturePlectis identity claims must be checked against current source, generated projections, and launch gates.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term Plectis --term-band context
    Open the current public-name vocabulary row.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface standards --band card --ids std_microcosm
    Open the governing standard and compatibility boundary.
Source refs
  • codex/standards/std_microcosm.json (identity_standard)
  • codex/doctrine/paper_modules/microcosm_substrate.md (paper_module)
  • sites/microcosm/index.html (staged_public_landing)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::plectis.

componentcomponentpublic_projectionA component is one bounded public object in Plectis: a source-linked unit with its own card, evidence line, source route, and scope limit.

bounded public object Component is the public-facing word for the bounded units that older source and technical records may still call components. A component card should say what the unit does, what evidence backs it, where its public source route is, and where its scope stops.

ScopeUse component for reader-facing public objects; keep component only when quoting source identifiers or compatibility records.

ExampleA component card names the unit, its evidence line, source route, and scope limit.

Not thisDo not treat the public word component as evidence that every source-level component identifier has been renamed.

Reader ruleUse component in public site copy, reader packets, glossary rows, and first-use definitions. Keep component only for historical or source-level identifiers that have not yet been renamed, and avoid treating the public word as proof that every underlying technical id has changed.

Also shown ascomponent card, bounded public object

Search handlesorgan, public object

Related termsbelongs_toplectisComponents are the public object units exposed by Plectis.translatesmicrocosmComponent is the public copy translation for legacy Microcosm object vocabulary.usesscope_limitEach component needs an explicit limit on what its evidence supports.
How to check
  • ./repo-python tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py --check --validate
    Check that component copy is generated and passes public vocabulary gates.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term component --term-band context
    Open the public component vocabulary row.
Source refs
  • tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py (public_projection_builder)
  • microcosm-substrate/core/organ_atlas.json (compatibility_source)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::component.

scope_limitscope limitpublic_projectionA scope limit is the public boundary that says what a component's evidence does not establish.

what evidence stops Scope limit is the public-facing term for the boundary line on a component, evidence class, or public packet. It keeps a real result from becoming a larger claim about launch readiness, whole-system correctness, hosted service behavior, professional advice, or private runtime state.

ScopeUse scope limit when a public result needs to say exactly what its evidence does not establish.

ExampleA component can show a local fixture pass while its scope limit rejects launch readiness or whole-system correctness.

Not thisDo not turn a passing result, high evidence rank, or working command into a broader claim without the matching scope evidence.

Reader ruleUse scope limit in public copy whenever a result, card, digest row, or AI answer needs to separate what is shown from what is not shown. Older internal records may still use scope limit or scope limit; public surfaces should say scope limit unless quoting an identifier.

Also shown asscope boundary, claim boundary

Search handlesclaim ceiling, authority ceiling

Related termsboundscomponentEach component carries a scope limit.protectsplectisPublic Plectis copy stays honest by keeping scope limits close to evidence.requiresliving_system_postureA scope limit must be read against current public records, not stale memory.
How to check
  • ./repo-python tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py --check --validate
    Check that generated public packets still expose claim boundaries safely.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term scope_limit --term-band context
    Open the public scope-limit vocabulary row.
Source refs
  • tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py (public_projection_builder)
  • microcosm-substrate/core/organ_registry.json (compatibility_source)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::scope_limit.

evidence_classevidence classevidenceAn evidence class names the kind of support a public result has, not the truth of every possible claim about it.

type of support Evidence class is the public label for the support category behind a card, result, or packet. It helps a reader distinguish source-linked rows, fixture passes, local tool runs, review packets, and other bounded support types.

ScopeUse evidence class to name what kind of support a public result has, before making any stronger claim from it.

ExampleA card can say a result is fixture-backed, source-linked, or locally runnable, then keep the scope limit close by.

Not thisDo not treat a high evidence rank or class label as proof of launch readiness, security, or whole-system correctness.

Reader ruleUse evidence class when a public page needs to say what backs a claim. Pair it with a scope limit, because the class names the support kind while the scope limit names what the support does not establish.

Also shown asevidence kind, evidence profile

Search handlesevidence rank, proof consumer class

Related termsboundsscope_limitEvidence type must be read with the claim boundary.classifiesrecordA record can carry an evidence class describing the support it contains.protectsplectisPlectis public copy uses evidence classes to avoid overclaiming.
How to check
  • jq '.' microcosm-substrate/core/organ_evidence_classes.json
    Read the source evidence-class registry.
  • ./repo-python tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py --check --validate
    Verify the public projection still carries bounded evidence classes.
Source refs
  • microcosm-substrate/core/organ_evidence_classes.json (source_registry)
  • tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py (public_projection_builder)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::evidence_class.

recordrecordartifactA record is an inspectable artifact or row that preserves a result, route, result record, or verification state.

inspectable artifact row Record is the public word for a durable artifact that can be reopened: a result row, result record, generated JSON output, review packet, or verification entry. A record should say what produced it and where its authority stops.

ScopeUse record for a concrete source or generated artifact that preserves what was checked, emitted, or routed.

ExampleA verification record can show the command, inputs, status, and scope limit for a public check.

Not thisDo not call a paragraph a record unless it points to a concrete artifact, row, or generated output.

Reader ruleUse record when the public site points to something more concrete than prose. Pair it with source refs and, when it supports a claim, an evidence class and scope limit.

Also shown asresult record, verification record

Search handlesreceipt, audit record, generated record

Related termscan_carryevidence_classA record can name the support type behind it.bounded_byscope_limitA record's claim boundary must travel with it.belowsource_authorityA generated record is not stronger than its source authority.
How to check
  • jq '.components[0]' sites/microcosm/content-graph.json
    Inspect a generated public record shape.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term record --term-band context
    Read the record vocabulary row.
Source refs
  • sites/microcosm/content-graph.json (generated_public_record_index)
  • tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py (public_projection_builder)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::record.

substratesubstratesubstrateA system is the underlying working base of files, routes, state, and tools that projections read from.

underlying working base System names the source layer beneath a generated page or packet: the files, standards, ledgers, tools, and state that a projection maps but does not replace.

ScopeUse system for the underlying files, routes, state, and tools that a public page is mapping over.

ExampleThe public site is a projection over a source system, not the source authority itself.

Not thisDo not use system as a mystical synonym for the whole project when a concrete file, route, or record is meant.

Reader ruleUse system in technical public contexts when the distinction between source authority and generated projection matters. If a concrete file or record is available, name that instead.

Also shown assource substrate, workflow substrate

Search handleslive substrate, runtime substrate

Related termscontainssource_authoritySource authority lives in system-owned records and tools.projected_byplectisPlectis is a public map over source system.contrasts_withrecordA record is one inspectable artifact inside or generated from system.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --paper-module microcosm_substrate
    Open the system doctrine surface.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term substrate --term-band context
    Read the system vocabulary row.
Source refs
  • codex/doctrine/paper_modules/microcosm_substrate.md (paper_module)
  • AGENTS.override.md (agent_seed)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::substrate.

source_authoritysource authoritygovernanceSource authority is the artifact, standard, tool, or ledger that owns a fact more strongly than its projections.

where truth is owned Source authority answers where a reader should go when prose, generated HTML, or an AI summary may have drifted. It is usually a source file, standard, validator, ledger, or generated structured source record with an owner contract.

ScopeUse source authority to name the artifact or tool that a generated page must stay below.

ExampleA glossary row can cite the vocabulary registry as source authority while the HTML remains a projection.

Not thisDo not treat a generated page, digest, or AI answer as source authority unless the governing standard says it is.

Reader ruleUse source authority whenever public copy says a page is a map, projection, record, or digest. The public page can route to authority; it does not become authority by summarizing it.

Also shown assource of record, governing source

Search handlessource owner, authority posture

Related termsgovernsrecordRecords point to the authority that owns their claim.boundsplectisPlectis public pages stay below source authority.requiresscope_limitAuthority and scope must be named together for public claims.
How to check
  • jq '.authority_model' codex/standards/std_system_term.json
    Read the authority model for vocabulary projections.
  • ./repo-python tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py --check --validate
    Verify generated public pages preserve source authority boundaries.
Source refs
  • codex/standards/std_system_term.json (standard)
  • tools/meta/dissemination/build_microcosm_public_site.py (public_projection_builder)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::source_authority.

organorgan (source identifier)compatibility_identifierAn component is the source-level runtime unit name behind some public components.

source-level runtime unit Component is a legacy/source identifier for bounded runtime units. Public pages should normally say component or name the concrete function, while preserving component only in source paths, ids, compatibility records, and deep technical context.

ScopeUse component only when quoting or explaining source-level identifiers; use component for public reader-facing objects.

ExampleA source ref may point to organ_registry.json, while the public page calls the displayed unit a component.

Not thisDo not use component as the main public object word when component or the concrete function is available.

Reader ruleUse component when a public reader needs to understand a source path such as organ_registry.json or an old id. Do not make it the primary public vocabulary for the site.

Also shown assource organ, organ id

Search handlesruntime organ, organ registry

Related termstranslated_publicly_ascomponentComponent is the preferred reader-facing word.belongs_tosource_sliceComponent ids may appear in the public source slice.bounded_byscope_limitSource-level component records still need claim boundaries.
How to check
  • jq 'keys[0:5]' microcosm-substrate/core/organ_registry.json
    Inspect source-level component registry ids.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term organ --term-band context
    Read the component compatibility term.

witnesscheck resultevidenceA witness is source-level evidence vocabulary; public copy should usually say check result or verification record.

source-linked check result Witness is an internal/source word for an evidence route or support surface. Public Plectis copy should prefer check result or verification record unless quoting a source identifier.

ScopeUse check result or verification record in public copy; keep witness only when explaining source-level evidence routes.

ExampleA component can link to a verification record that shows what command or source row supports it.

Not thisDo not use witness to imply legal testimony, proof certification, endorsement, or whole-system correctness.

Reader ruleUse witness only when a source file, axiom-support route, or compatibility record uses that word. For public readers, translate to the concrete result, command, or record and keep the scope limit attached.

Also shown asverification record, witness

Search handlespublic witness, proof witness, witness route, witness surface

Related termspublicly_preferrecordVerification record is the safer reader-facing object.usesevidence_classA check result can carry an evidence class.bounded_byscope_limitA witness/check result must not exceed its claim boundary.
How to check
  • jq '.support_cases[0]?' microcosm-substrate/receipts/first_wave/axiom_support_cover/axiom_support_cover_result.json
    Inspect a generated support record when present.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term witness --term-band context
    Read the witness compatibility term.

navigablenavigablenavigationNavigable means a reader or agent has a concrete route to inspect the object instead of guessing from prose.

has a route Navigable is a property contract: an object has an anchor, route, source ref, relationship edge, command, or packet pointer that lets a reader move from summary to evidence.

ScopeUse navigable when a term, record, or page has a concrete route a reader or agent can follow.

ExampleA glossary row is navigable when it has an anchor, source refs, relationships, and evidence commands.

Not thisDo not call something navigable if it only sounds important but has no route, owner, or source ref.

Reader ruleUse navigable when judging whether a public surface can actually be followed. The useful question is not whether the word appears, but whether the page gives a route to the object and its authority.

Also shown asroutable, discoverable

Search handlesrouteable, navigability

Related termsrequiresroutingNavigable objects need routes.protectscold_agentNavigability prevents cold readers from guessing.usesrecordRecords make route targets inspectable.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --paper-module navigation_hologram_theory
    Open the navigation theory.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term navigable --term-band context
    Read the navigable vocabulary row.

above_and_beyondabove and beyondjudgment_traitAbove-and-beyond is Type A bounded completion: finish the local task, then close the discoverability, validation, and up-propagation loop that the task exposed.

close the durable loop Above-and-beyond does not mean speculative scope expansion. It means the controller notices the useful extra work that makes the result durable for the next cold agent: route the new surface, update the owning skill or paper module, add the vocabulary alias if the user's phrase would otherwise miss, run focused validation, and leave the result record.

ScopeUse above_and_beyond when a user asks for care, passion, common sense, or extra effort and the local task exposes a bounded follow-through obligation. The move is to do the smallest durable improvement that prevents rediscovery or stale guidance, while naming the boundary so the work does not become an unbounded campaign.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/local_to_general_propagation.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse above_and_beyond when a user asks for care, passion, common sense, or extra effort and the local task exposes a bounded follow-through obligation. The move is to do the smallest durable improvement that prevents rediscovery or stale guidance, while naming the boundary so the work does not become an unbounded campaign.

Also shown asgo above and beyond, going above and beyond

Search handlesabove-and-beyond, beyond the ask, extra mile, go further, overdeliver

Related termsspecializestype_a_judgmentAbove-and-beyond is one routeable Type A judgment trait.balancespassionVision can justify follow-through only when it stays source-backed and bounded.balancescareCare turns extra effort into upkeep for future agents and humans.operates_throughcommon_senseCommon sense chooses the smallest truthful improvement instead of broadening scope reflexively.usesup_propagateReusable above-and-beyond work moves into the owning durable plane.bounded_bystandardValidation, authority, and projection standards cap the extra work.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term above_and_beyond --term-band context
    Read above-and-beyond as a bounded Type A judgment trait.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --docs-route "go above and beyond care passion common sense"
    Route the phrase through the Type A operating-register read set.

actor_axesactor axesactorActor axes separate system type, class tier, surface, delegation role, authority, context mode, lane, and thinking budget before a Type A/B handoff decision is made.

actor classification axes Actor axes are the canonical Type A/B concept in con_016. Type A/B is system authority; low/middle/high capability, invocation surface, topology role, evidence access, purpose lane, and reasoning budget are independent classifications.

ScopeUse actor_axes whenever a prompt, route, or handoff says Type A, Type B, bridge, provider, operator HUD, worker, subagent, or delegated. Resolve system authority first: tool-using native subagents with live repo affordances are delegated Type A; operator-HUD, bridge, provider/API, NIM/OpenRouter, public web, and injected-context cognition are Type B unless live private-system authority is present.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/standards/std_agent_entry_surface.json::type_b_to_type_a_handoff_framing_contract.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse actor_axes whenever a prompt, route, or handoff says Type A, Type B, bridge, provider, operator HUD, worker, subagent, or delegated. Resolve system authority first: tool-using native subagents with live repo affordances are delegated Type A; operator-HUD, bridge, provider/API, NIM/OpenRouter, public web, and injected-context cognition are Type B unless live private-system authority is present.

Also shown asactor_axes, Type A/B actor axes

Search handlescon_016::actor_axes, substrate authority axes, type a type b concept

Related termsdefinestype_aType A is substrate_type=type_a under actor axes.definestype_bType B is substrate_type=type_b under actor axes.clarifiesbridgeBridge is one Type B surface, not the whole Type B concept.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface concepts --band card --ids con_016
    Open the canonical Type A/B actor-axis concept.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term actor_axes --term-band context
    Read the vocabulary compression of the actor-axis concept.

affordance_rowaffordance rowartifactAn affordance row is a standard-owned cognitive-asset row carrying identity, decision affordance, cost, evidence, local edges, lifecycle state, and worker history.

standard-owned decision handle Affordance rows are the navigation plane's atomic unit: each artifact kind exposes itself as rows with bands, not as monolithic files, so a cold agent can choose the next read from a budgeted option surface instead of grepping source.

ScopeUse affordance-row language when designing browse surfaces, compact views, lenses, or navigation hologram groups. Every standard should know how to emit its artifacts as rows at flag / card / context / evidence bands with source-fingerprinted evidence commands; rows are what standards compile, lenses project, skills move between, and result records record against.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse affordance-row language when designing browse surfaces, compact views, lenses, or navigation hologram groups. Every standard should know how to emit its artifacts as rows at flag / card / context / evidence bands with source-fingerprinted evidence commands; rows are what standards compile, lenses project, skills move between, and result records record against.

Also shown ascognitive asset row, standard-owned affordance row

Search handlesnavigation row, option row, choice row

Related termscomposeshologramHolograms are ordered affordance-row sets.emitted_bystandardStandards compile artifacts into affordance rows.projects_throughbandEach row exposes itself at several bands.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --paper-module navigation_hologram_theory
    Open the affordance-ledger theory roof.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --nav-hologram
    Browse compiled affordance rows at group / row / preview bands.

agent_seedagent seedsubstrateAgent seed is the attributed AI-authored system for durable synthesis that must not impersonate operator voice.

agent authored durable synthesis Agent seed stores agent-authored observations, synthesis, and drift signals under attribution. It is useful durable system, but it is lower authority than source note for operator intent.

ScopeUse agent seed when the agent itself learned something worth preserving and the material is not Will voice, not immediate doctrine, and not just private memory. Preserve authorship and source refs.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/agent_seed_substrate.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse agent seed when the agent itself learned something worth preserving and the material is not Will voice, not immediate doctrine, and not just private memory. Preserve authorship and source refs.

Also shown asagent_seed, agent_seed.md, agent-authored seed

annexannexsubstrateAn reference is curated external system the repo adapts (not adopts) under pri_080, keeping local authority while preserving reusable patterns.

curated external substrate References live under annexes/ and carry shape-level notes (annex_family.json, annex_contents.json, annex_notes.json) describing external frameworks, repos, or documents at pattern level. They preempt re-derivation by letting the controller check prior work before inventing locally.

ScopeUse reference language when the task shape is likely present in external prior art. Route via ./repo-python annex_import.py route --problem "..." or kernel.py --reference-inspiration before authoring; translate the pattern into local idiom rather than importing system. Reference notes describe the reference in its own terms; consumption is always situated. A populated reference should support transfer, upgrade-ours, hybridization, or explicit rejection when a local seam asks for it. If the task is primitive-choice, architecture, memory, navigation, standard, skill, agent behavior, or failure-class repair, treat the reference corpus as a compressed prior-art pressure field rather than only reopening the references named in the immediate chat.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/codex_annex_substrate.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse reference language when the task shape is likely present in external prior art. Route via ./repo-python annex_import.py route --problem "..." or kernel.py --reference-inspiration before authoring; translate the pattern into local idiom rather than importing system. Reference notes describe the reference in its own terms; consumption is always situated. A populated reference should support transfer, upgrade-ours, hybridization, or explicit rejection when a local seam asks for it. If the task is primitive-choice, architecture, memory, navigation, standard, skill, agent behavior, or failure-class repair, treat the reference corpus as a compressed prior-art pressure field rather than only reopening the references named in the immediate chat.

Also shown asannex note, annex substrate

Search handlescurated external, adapted substrate, annex family, pattern fuel, annex prior art, annex prior-art pressure, prior art pressure field

Related termsadapted_intostandardReference patterns can crystallize into local standards.adapted_intoskillReference patterns can refine local skills.contrasts_withraw_seedReference is external pattern evidence; source note is operator voice.pressuresfailure_class_propagationReference patterns can help classify whether a local failure implies a broader repair class.
How to check
  • ./repo-python annex_import.py route --problem "<task>"
    Route to shape-matched references before inventing.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --annex-inspiration "<task>"
    Compressed reference prior-pattern packet.

  • codex/doctrine/skills/annex/annex_pattern_transfer.md (skill)
  • codex/standards/annex/std_annex_notes.json (standard)

Generated from the governed system vocabulary registry: codex/doctrine/system_vocabulary/term_registry.json::annex.

appeal_lifecycleappeal lifecyclegovernanceAppeal lifecycle is the governed path from a challenged judgment to classification, repair-plane ownership, rationale, and resolution.

judgment challenge lifecycle An appeal is not a failed row. It is a internal control challenge to a judgment or expected label. Pending appeals can block affected promotion until resolved with a rationale and, where needed, a repair-plane test.

ScopeUse appeal_lifecycle when Operator Court, route judgment, or projection checks emit unresolved challenges. The current appeal ledger exists, but lifecycle enforcement is not yet built; the Workingness Instrument names the missing contract.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/workingness_instrument.md::Microcosm Trace.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse appeal_lifecycle when Operator Court, route judgment, or projection checks emit unresolved challenges. The current appeal ledger exists, but lifecycle enforcement is not yet built; the Workingness Instrument names the missing contract.

applyapplyruntimeApply is the controlled mutation lane that turns reviewed plans or operations into repo changes.

execute verified changes Apply is the execution side of observe/apply. It should act on specified targets, validate before and after, and preserve rollback or evidence where the lane supports it.

ScopeUse apply only when the target, operation, and verification surface are clear. Do not let observe outputs silently become mutations without an apply contract or controller decision.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/kernel/apply.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse apply only when the target, operation, and verification surface are clear. Do not let observe outputs silently become mutations without an apply contract or controller decision.

Also shown asapply lane, apply plan, mutation

artifact_kindartifact kindartifactArtifact kind is the stable class of repo object exposed by Kind Atlas before row-level browsing.

artifact class Artifact kind names the class of thing being browsed, such as skills, system_terms, compression_profiles, paper_modules, standards, principles, or frontend_views.

ScopeUse artifact-kind language before choosing rows. Kind Atlas names the available classes and their support status; the option-surface adapter then expands supported kinds into stable ids, card rows, and source evidence.

ExampleRepresentative source: system/lib/kind_atlas.py.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

axiomaxiomdoctrineAn axiom is a candidate nearly-mathematical always-true rule that constrains principles rather than being one.

nearly-mathematical system rule Axioms sit above principles in the doctrine lattice: principles interpret axioms into rules-of-behavior in bounded contexts, whereas axioms are formal declarations the whole system is built to preserve. The repo targets roughly seven, still provisional.

ScopeUse axiom language when the claim is invariant across every context the system operates in — voice authority, projection discipline, lattice connectivity, operator scope, or primitive choice. Candidate axioms route through the axiom-candidate lane; they do not become pri_* and they must never be hand-minted in a skill body.

ExampleRepresentative source: CLAUDE.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse axiom language when the claim is invariant across every context the system operates in — voice authority, projection discipline, lattice connectivity, operator scope, or primitive choice. Candidate axioms route through the axiom-candidate lane; they do not become pri_* and they must never be hand-minted in a skill body.

Also shown asaxiom candidate, pri_axiom

Search handlesinfinity stone, system axiom, primitive constraint

Related termsconstrainsprincipleAxioms constrain the principle layer.belongs_todoctrineAxioms are the upper doctrine layer.governed_bylatticeAxioms are the root of the governance lattice.

    Route the axiom-candidate surfaces.

    Root ontology discusses axiom layer.

bandbandnavigationA band is a strict packet schema for one level of compression, not a prose length.

one compression level packet Bands are the named compression tiers of a Russian-doll/telescope row (word / phrase / flag / card / context / deep / evidence, per profile). Each band declares what it must carry, what it forbids, and what drilldown it points to.

ScopeUse band language when specifying how an artifact should project itself for browse vs working vs authority reads. Every compression profile declares its bands and their contracts; creators emit at bands, navigators ask for a band, and drift shows up as stale-fingerprint flags on a specific band rather than on the whole file.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse band language when specifying how an artifact should project itself for browse vs working vs authority reads. Every compression profile declares its bands and their contracts; creators emit at bands, navigators ask for a band, and drift shows up as stale-fingerprint flags on a specific band rather than on the whole file.

Also shown ascompression band, band schema

Search handlesband ladder, russian doll band, definition band, telescope band, telescoping band, holographic tier

Related termsdeclared_bycompression_profileA compression profile declares its legal bands and band contracts.projectsaffordance_rowBands are how an affordance row shows itself at different costs.used_byhologramHologram nodes render rows through a requested band.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term <term> --term-band context
    Resolve a term at a specific band.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --paper-module navigation_hologram_theory
    Read the bands-as-schemas refinement in situ.

bridgebridgeruntimeBridge sends bounded context to an external worker and receives a response; iteration belongs to the controller or mission runner.

single-call external worker lane Bridge is the external worker lane for tasks reducible to context plus instructions plus response. It is powerful for parallel reasoning, research, and population work, but it only sees what the controller injects.

ScopeUse bridge when a bounded worker packet can be answered without direct repo agency. The controller owns context selection, schema, import/readback, validation, and any mutation decision. Bridge output is candidate evidence unless the owning lane promotes it.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/bridge_runtime.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse bridge when a bounded worker packet can be answered without direct repo agency. The controller owns context selection, schema, import/readback, validation, and any mutation decision. Bridge output is candidate evidence unless the owning lane promotes it.

Also shown asbridge runtime, bridge dispatch, external worker

Search handlesbrowser bridge

Related termsoperates_underobserveBridge often powers observe/probe passes.controlled_bykernelKernel and controller surfaces launch and read bridge work.

    Open bridge runtime ontology.

    Open bridge dispatch authoring skill.

carecarejudgment_traitCare is a Type A judgment trait that treats the system's future navigability, correctness, and human stakes as part of the work surface.

stake becomes upkeep Care supports Type A judgment by making maintenance, continuity, and downstream agent comprehension visible as obligations. It is not a decorative value word; it should route to source-backed behavior like fixing route gaps, preserving provenance, avoiding adapter bloat, and leaving explicit completion result records.

ScopeUse care when the local move should protect the next human or agent from re-deriving context, inheriting stale facts, or losing the user's intent. Care can justify up-propagation, validation, and clarity work, but it remains a judgment trait under standards rather than free-floating prose.

ExampleRepresentative source: CODEX.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse care when the local move should protect the next human or agent from re-deriving context, inheriting stale facts, or losing the user's intent. Care can justify up-propagation, validation, and clarity work, but it remains a judgment trait under standards rather than free-floating prose.

Also shown assystem care, agent care

Search handlescare directive, care register, care posture, care and passion

Related termssupportstype_a_judgmentCare is one trait supporting live controller judgment.operates_throughcommon_senseCare becomes practical through situated judgment and cheap truthful surfaces.routes_toup_propagateCare-driven local lessons should move to the owning plane when reusable.

  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term care --term-band context
    Read care as a routeable judgment trait.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --docs-route "common sense care passion type A judgment"

cold_agentcold agentactorA cold agent is a capable Type A agent that starts without conversation context and must orient from disk.

unprimed capable agent Cold agent is the canonical reader the repo designs for. Skills, paper modules, CLAUDE.md, and bootstrap surfaces assume the reader has no prior-session memory, no tribal knowledge, and no guess about which phrase resolves which route.

ScopeUse cold-agent language when deciding what belongs in an entry surface vs a paper module vs a source note paragraph. The test is always: can a cold agent open this and make the next safe decision without re-deriving the system? If not, add the missing affordance rather than leaving folklore in place.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

common_sensecommon sensejudgment_traitCommon sense is Type A situated judgment that uses the cheapest truthful surface, notices route gaps or drift, and leaves a better generalized surface behind.

situated repair judgment Common sense is not ungoverned instinct. In this repo it is the live judgment metabolism that reads the surrounding doctrine, applies ordinary intelligence to the local case, tests whether the noun or route exists, and up-propagates reusable lessons into the owning artifact.

ScopeUse common_sense when a cold agent should infer the right action from axioms, principles, standards, entry-surface rules, and current system rather than waiting for a bespoke instruction. If the action reveals a reusable missing noun, route, standard, or skill move, repair the shared surface and cite the source case.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/local_to_general_propagation.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

If the action reveals a reusable missing noun, route, standard, or skill move, repair the shared surface and cite the source case.

compatibility_routecompatibility routeroutingA compatibility route keeps an old URL or identifier useful while pointing readers to the current surface.

old path support Compatibility route names the migration behavior for old links: tombstone pages, redirects, aliases, and manifests that preserve access without preserving the old name as current.

ScopeUse compatibility route for an old URL or identifier that remains supported only to get readers to the current Plectis surface.

ExampleThe old Microcosm site root can show a tombstone and route readers to the Plectis site.

Not thisDo not treat a compatibility route as proof that the old name is still the current public brand.

Reader ruleUse compatibility route for Microcosm-era URLs, source paths, and identifiers that must continue resolving. Pair it with lineage language so the old route cannot read as a second active site.

Also shown aslegacy route, tombstone route

Search handlesold route, redirect route, migration route

Related termsroutes_frommicrocosmMicrocosm is a legacy/compatibility label.routes_toplectisCompatibility routes point to the current public identity.usesroutingCompatibility behavior is a routing concern.

compressioncompressionnavigationCompression is governed preservation through declared bands, profiles, drilldowns, omission result records, and selective telescoping.

preserve through bands Compression turns a larger system into a smaller row without breaking authority. It must name what was preserved, what was omitted, which profile/band governed the row, and how navigation can drill back to source.

ScopeUse compression when a prompt asks to summarize, populate bands, reduce context, prepare worker packets, or make a surface navigable. The safe route is profile first: choose the artifact kind, governing standard/profile, band, dependency neighborhood, omission result record, and validation contract before wording the compact row.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/compression/profile_governed_compression.md.

compression_profilecompression profileartifactA compression profile is the governed rulebook declaring an artifact kind's bands, contracts, mandatory preservation, allowed loss, and forbidden collapses.

governed compression rulebook Compression profiles make compression a typed transformation instead of ad-hoc summarization. Each profile names the creator skill, navigator skill, source ladder, band schemas, evidence policy, and validation probe so cheap workers, controllers, and humans all compress coherently.

ScopeUse compression-profile language when authoring or refining how an artifact kind projects through bands. Reach for a profile before collapsing nuance: the profile tells you what must survive, what may drop, and what counts as illegal collapse; if the current profile does not fit the case, refine it through the registry rather than inventing local shape.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

conceptconceptdoctrineA concept names a stable object or idea in the system ontology.

named system object Concepts are doctrine nouns. They identify reusable objects, abstractions, or classes of meaning that mechanisms and principles can relate to.

ScopeUse concepts when the task asks what a thing is or how a named object fits into the system. Concepts should be curated, source-linked, and related to mechanisms and principles.

critic_posturecritic posturejudgment_traitCritic posture is the Type A judgment trait that turns critique into a reusable test for weak abstractions, route gaps, and missing plane homes.

critique becomes thinking trait Critic posture does not require spawning a separate critic role by default. In human-AI brainstorming it is a thinking trait: notice when a theory turn exposes a route gap, name the candidate primitive, probe --term and --docs-route, then choose the plane home that would make the lesson repeatable.

ScopeUse critic_posture when the work needs an internal failure question: what would a cold agent miss, what route currently fails, what standard is absent, or what local case is being overgeneralized? If the critique stabilizes, it should refine vocabulary, a skill move, a standard, or an axiom candidate rather than remain a chat objection.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/human_ai_brainstorming.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

If the critique stabilizes, it should refine vocabulary, a skill move, a standard, or an axiom candidate rather than remain a chat objection.

doctrinedoctrinedoctrineDoctrine is the curated layer of principles, concepts, mechanisms, routes, and interpretation surfaces.

curated system meaning layer Doctrine keeps the system's reusable meaning explicit. It translates raw voice, evidence, standards, skills, and implementation lessons into named, searchable, governed artifacts.

ScopeUse doctrine when a task asks why the system is shaped a certain way, what principle governs it, or how concepts and mechanisms relate. Do not treat doctrine as source code authority when implementation evidence is needed.

epistemic_control_planeepistemic control planeruntimeAn epistemic control plane binds what the system believes and does not know to what actions are allowed, blocked, or escalated.

belief-bound action control The epistemic control plane is the future runtime form of the Workingness Instrument. It would compile result records, judgments, appeals, and projection checks into claim rows and action boundaries.

ScopeUse epistemic control plane for the runtime version of workingness. In the current repo it is only an alias and future surface; the authored artifact is the Workingness Instrument paper module plus candidate standard.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/workingness_instrument.md.

failure_class_propagationfailure class propagationpropagationFailure class propagation treats a local break as a sample of a possible broader class until a bounded sibling scan proves otherwise.

bounded class repair Failure class propagation is the disciplined version of 'do not just patch this one thing.' It names the local case, hypothesizes the class, checks sibling surfaces, names the not-when boundary, and routes the bounded repair or rejection to the owning plane.

ScopeUse this term when a bug, drift, bad local read, missing route, malformed standard, or agent misunderstanding might recur across other surfaces. The valid packet carries local case, sibling scan, class boundary, overgeneralization counter-force, plane home, and validation. The invalid moves are one-off patching with no class thought, or widening into a campaign without a boundary.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/local_to_general_propagation.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

The valid packet carries local case, sibling scan, class boundary, overgeneralization counter-force, plane home, and validation. The invalid moves are one-off patching with no class thought, or widening into a campaign without a boundary.

Also shown asfailure_class_propagation, class of failure, failure class

generalise_up_propagategeneralise-up-propagatepropagationGeneralise-up-propagate turns a local lesson into a portable invariant before choosing its durable plane home.

local lesson becomes general Generalise-up-propagation is up-propagation with abstraction discipline. It preserves the local case while naming the broader lesson, applicability boundary, and not-when boundary.

ScopeUse this term when the point is not merely to save a lesson, but to transpose it across the system lattice. The local case remains evidence; the generalized row must not pretend one instance is universal without boundaries.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/local_to_general_propagation.md.

hologramhologramnavigationA hologram is a generated or authored projection that lets a small row carry enough of the whole to choose a drilldown.

compressed whole from parts Hologram means a source-derived compression surface with nested bands and evidence commands. Telescope, Russian doll, and holographic compression name the same tiered-compression behavior: read the smallest useful layer, then selectively expand the shard or facet that matters.

ScopeUse hologram surfaces for broad comprehension before semantic search or source reads. Treat hologram output as a read interface, source-linked only; follow evidence commands when decisions need proof. Do not split telescope into a separate primitive unless the owning standard/profile shows a different contract.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse hologram surfaces for broad comprehension before semantic search or source reads. Treat hologram output as a read interface, source-linked only; follow evidence commands when decisions need proof. Do not split telescope into a separate primitive unless the owning standard/profile shows a different contract.

Also shown asnavigation hologram, holographic, compiled hologram

Search handlesRussian doll, telescope, telescoping, holographic telescope

Related termsservesnavigationHolograms are navigation surfaces.compressesdoctrineDoctrine can be projected into hologram rows.

    Open navigation hologram root.

    Open theory roof.

kernelkernelruntimeKernel is the repo-local command spine that exposes navigation, phase, system, observe, apply, and status surfaces.

repo control query spine Kernel is the CLI-facing control and query layer. It gives agents bounded JSON packets and legal moves before they widen into files or mutate state.

ScopeUse kernel commands first when the task asks where something lives, what is active, what to read, how to route a subsystem, or how to inspect current runtime state. Kernel output is often a view profile over deeper source authority.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/kernel_entry_paths.md.

Also shown askernel.py, repo kernel, control spine

laboratorylaboratoryruntimeA laboratory is the old microcosm-as-experiment lane: a controlled, fixture-backed probe used before live system migration.

controlled experiment substrate A laboratory is a disposable or self-hosted experiment lane that exercises candidate standards, compression profiles, row jobs, result records, routes, validators, or product mechanics on a bounded fixture. Laboratory output is evidence and promotion candidates, never authority.

ScopeUse laboratory language when the task proposes a new standard, profile, row shape, lens, result record, validator, or runtime contract and needs an evidence lane before broad migration. Legacy system_microcosm_probe ids still point here for compatibility.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/standards/std_laboratory.json.

latticelatticeontologyA lattice is the typed network of system planes, artifacts, routes, standards, skills, principles, runtime surfaces, and result records.

typed planes and edges Lattice names the whole-system relationship graph that lets a lesson move across artifact kinds without losing authority. It is broader than doctrine alone: standards, skills, paper modules, source note projections, vocabulary, navigation, runtime, and result records all participate.

ScopeUse lattice when the question is about how a local lesson, term, route, or standard connects across planes. The lattice is the coordinate system for choosing whether a lesson belongs in vocabulary, docs-route, a skill, a paper module, a standard, PEER, agent seed, or an apply lane.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

lattice_transpositionlattice transpositionpropagationLattice transposition strips a local lesson to its reusable invariant and routes it to the plane where other artifact classes can inherit it.

portable invariant routing Lattice transposition is generalized up-propagation across the system lattice. It preserves the concrete source case, names the portable invariant, declares applicability and not_when boundaries, then deposits the lesson in the narrowest mature plane: skill, standard, paper module, vocabulary, PEER row, agent seed, axiom candidate, or principle lane.

ScopeUse this term when a local situation reveals a rule that should not stay local to that file or task. A principle-promotion friction can refine the principle standard; a standards edit can refine standards-authoring discipline; a routing failure can refine a skill and docs route. The move is invalid if it loses the source case or jumps authority gates.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/local_to_general_propagation.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse this term when a local situation reveals a rule that should not stay local to that file or task. A principle-promotion friction can refine the principle standard; a standards edit can refine standards-authoring discipline; a routing failure can refine a skill and docs route. The move is invalid if it loses the source case or jumps authority gates.

Also shown aslattice-transposition, generalized up-propagation, generalised up-propagation

living_system_postureliving system posturepostureLiving system posture reads every artifact as currentness-bearing, not timeless.

read with currentness Living system posture is the stance that result records, generated surfaces, raw voice, doctrine, standards, and runtime claims are evidence from a time and regime. Old claims stay preserved, but current authority requires checking live system and freshness surfaces.

ScopeUse living-system-posture language when a local lesson reveals that an old result record, generated packet, source note statement, candidate doctrine row, or CLI claim may no longer describe the current system. The repair is not to erase history; it is to preserve the old claim as evidence, check live system, and up-propagate the generalized currentness boundary to the owning surface.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/local_to_general_propagation.md.

mechanismmechanismdoctrineA mechanism explains how a system object moves, transforms, routes, validates, or changes state.

system movement pattern Mechanisms are doctrine verbs or processes. They connect concepts, principles, skills, standards, and code loci through an explanation of how something happens.

metabolismmetabolismruntimeMetabolism keeps derived surfaces fresh and useful by running governed maintenance, population, validation, and result record loops over authority system.

governed substrate upkeep Metabolism is the system's governed upkeep loop. It observes authority system, queues bounded work, populates or refreshes derived surfaces, validates outputs, records result records, and stops at the right authority gate.

ScopeUse metabolism when the work is not a one-off answer but durable surface maintenance: standard enforcement, Russian-doll compression population, projection refresh, source note settled sync, row patch drafting, provider capability refresh, or scaffold repair. Navigation should read metabolized projections and flag stale surfaces; metabolism should populate or refresh them outside the default read path.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/continuous_runtime_layer.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse metabolism when the work is not a one-off answer but durable surface maintenance: standard enforcement, Russian-doll compression population, projection refresh, source note settled sync, row patch drafting, provider capability refresh, or scaffold repair. Navigation should read metabolized projections and flag stale surfaces; metabolism should populate or refresh them outside the default read path.

Also shown asmetabolic system, metabolism lane, generalized metabolism

navigationnavigationnavigationNavigation exposes bounded choices and drilldowns so agents see what exists before paying full-source context.

browse choices before source Navigation is the browse-and-drilldown layer over system system. It favors contents pages, option rows, bands, and evidence commands over blind grep or full-file reads.

ScopeUse navigation when a cold agent needs to move from a broad question to a reliable read set. The navigation ladder includes pulse, phase, paper-module lookup, docs-route, navigation hologram, source note navigation, locate, compile, and source evidence.

observeobserveruntimeObserve gathers structured evidence and responses before implementation or doctrine mutation.

gather evidence before deciding Observe is the evidence-gathering side of observe/apply. It can be local or bridge-backed and should produce bounded artifacts that later planning or application can trust.

ScopeUse observe when the next honest step is to learn, map, compare, or probe rather than mutate. Observe artifacts should preserve questions, target files, responses, synthesis, and validation signals.

omission_receiptomission receiptnavigationAn omission result record records what a compact row leaves out and how to recover the source authority.

named missing context An omission result record is the row-level truth boundary that prevents compressed navigation from pretending to be complete; it names omitted material, explains why it is omitted, and gives a drilldown command.

ScopeUse omission-result record language whenever a flag or card row compresses source material. The result record should name the omitted source bodies, second-order relationships, native/deeper bands, or legacy command output and point to the command that reopens the authority surface.

ExampleRepresentative source: system/lib/standard_option_surface.py.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

operator_proxyoperator proxyruntime_roleOperator proxy is the Bridge role where a Type B worker carries Will's message-mission intent through an injected packet without becoming operator authority.

bounded operator-intent proxy Use operator_proxy when Bridge receives enough context, doctrine, examples, schema, and constraints to act as a projection of the operator for a bounded message mission. The proxy is real, but bounded: Type A still owns context choice, model-tier selection, validation, import, and promotion.

ScopeThe conscious/subconscious framing means operator voice and Type A judgment choose purpose and authority, while Bridge packets, prompt manifests, model-tier controls, result records, and caches are subconscious infrastructure. The subconscious serves by making intent executable; it does not override the conscious layer.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/bridge_operator_proxy.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleThe conscious/subconscious framing means operator voice and Type A judgment choose purpose and authority, while Bridge packets, prompt manifests, model-tier controls, result records, and caches are subconscious infrastructure. The subconscious serves by making intent executable; it does not override the conscious layer.

Also shown asoperator_proxy, subconscious bridge, message mission proxy

operator_voiceoperator voicepostureOperator voice is the unmediated first-person expression of the operator (Will), distinct from agent-authored projections and from Bridge proxy output.

direct operator expression Operator voice names the posture of direct, unmediated Will intent — source notes, voiced paragraphs, and explicit operator instructions that carry authority over all projections. Contrast with operator_proxy (Bridge acting on behalf of the operator under a prepared packet) and with agent_authored_projection (agents compressing operator voice into doctrine).

ScopeOperator voice shows up in source note paragraphs, direct verbal instructions, and voiced complaints. The distillation pipeline (PD1) exists precisely to preserve operator voice: extract intent without dumping text, keep motifs verbatim, split reversals. When Will authors directly, the voice-preservation rule applies as-voiced; when AI mediates, the gesture is transcribed into technical clarity.

ExampleUse this term only where the linked source record uses or governs it.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleOperator voice shows up in source note paragraphs, direct verbal instructions, and voiced complaints. The distillation pipeline (PD1) exists precisely to preserve operator voice: extract intent without dumping text, keep motifs verbatim, split reversals. When Will authors directly, the voice-preservation rule applies as-voiced; when AI mediates, the gesture is transcribed into technical clarity.

Also shown aswill voice, direct voice

Search handlesvoice authority

option_surfaceoption surfacenavigationAn option surface is a standard-owned row set for choosing artifacts by kind and stable id before opening source authority.

selectable row surface An option surface is the rung-1 browse surface behind Kind Atlas: it enumerates rows at cheap bands, drills selected ids to card rows, and preserves evidence/currentness/omission boundaries without replacing source authority.

ScopeUse option-surface language when the agent needs a selectable catalog rather than keyword search. The option surface owns adapter-supported bands such as flag/card, carries missing-id result records for selected ids, and points to legacy or source commands as drilldown rather than treating them as primary browse.

ExampleRepresentative source: system/lib/standard_option_surface.py.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

The option surface owns adapter-supported bands such as flag/card, carries missing-id result records for selected ids, and points to legacy or source commands as drilldown rather than treating them as primary browse.

Also shown asoption_surface, standard option surface

paper_modulepaper moduleartifactA paper module is the cold-readable subsystem ontology that preempts re-derivation for a known area.

subsystem ontology packet Paper modules are subsystem-level explanation packets. They summarize intent, shape, invariants, loci, current state, deliverables, gaps, and refresh rules for an area of the repo.

ScopeUse paper modules before reopening source for an existing subsystem. If the module is stale or missing, refresh or author it through the paper-module lanes rather than leaving the lesson in chat.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/standards/std_paper_module.json.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

passionpassionjudgment_traitPassion is a Type A judgment trait that keeps the live vision and stakes present while still routing through standards, evidence, and source authority.

vision activates work Passion supports Type A judgment by preventing the system from flattening operator intent into sterile task completion. It belongs in shared grammar when it changes how future agents preserve vision, urgency, or meaning, but it must remain source-backed and constrained by standards.

ScopeUse passion when the user's live theory, ambition, or emotional stake is part of what makes the correct action intelligible. The repair is not to paste passionate prose into adapters; it is to route the register through vocabulary, human-AI brainstorming, paper modules, and the entry-surface standard when cold agents need the affordance.

ExampleRepresentative source: CODEX.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse passion when the user's live theory, ambition, or emotional stake is part of what makes the correct action intelligible. The repair is not to paste passionate prose into adapters; it is to route the register through vocabulary, human-AI brainstorming, paper modules, and the entry-surface standard when cold agents need the affordance.

Also shown aspassion register, vision activation

Search handlesoperator stake, why this matters, care and passion

Related termssupportstype_a_judgmentPassion is one trait supporting live controller judgment.balancescarePassion keeps vision active while care keeps upkeep explicit.constrained_bystandardPassion must route through standards rather than adapter-only prose.

path_handlepath handlenavigationA path handle is a compact id that expands to a storage path plus role, label, fingerprint, redirects, and evidence command.

compact expandable path id Path handles keep long or unstable storage paths out of compact packets while preserving source-authoritative expansion. They are the safer first layer before broad filesystem renames.

ScopeUse path_handle when repeated long paths waste context or when a storage rename would be risky. A handle can improve readability immediately while leaving the filesystem path and old references intact until a migration proof exists.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/semantic_naming_grammar.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse path_handle when repeated long paths waste context or when a storage rename would be risky. A handle can improve readability immediately while leaving the filesystem path and old references intact until a migration proof exists.

Also shown assemantic path handle, path alias, path alias registry

peerpeeractorA peer is a future capable Codex or Claude agent that can inherit source-linked lessons from earlier agents.

future capable agent sibling Peer language preserves agent-to-agent authority. A peer can use candidate lessons, but should not mistake them for operator voice or active doctrine.

ScopeUse peer when the lesson is for future Type A agents operating the repo. Peer material belongs in PEER result records or agent-authored systems until promoted through owning lanes.

peer_propagatepeer-propagatepropagationPeer-propagate means a capable agent records a source-linked candidate lesson for future peer agents.

peer teaches future peers Peer-propagation is the PEER lane: one Type A agent preserves reusable operating judgment for future Codex or Claude peers without promoting it to operator voice or active doctrine.

ScopeUse peer-propagate when the lesson is real and useful but not mature enough for a skill, standard, paper module, agent seed, axiom candidate, or principle apply lane. The row should cite source refs, applicability, not_when, bands, and promotion state.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/peer_propagation.md.

phasephaseruntimeA phase is a scoped work packet inside a family, with synth, scaffold, wave, evidence, and completion state.

bounded family work packet Phase is the repo's bounded execution unit for substantial work. It preserves the current objective, execution mode, active wave, references, and legal next steps.

ScopeUse kernel.py --phase before substantial work to recover the active phase and decide the lightest execution mode. Do not infer current state from memory when phase files and kernel views exist.

principleprincipledoctrineA principle is a durable operating law promoted through doctrine curation, not an ad hoc session slogan.

durable operating law Principles encode general commitments that constrain agent behavior and artifact design. They also proxy design intuition: the primitive, taste, and tradeoff a future agent should reuse when the live case differs from the original evidence.

ScopeUse principles to understand why a pattern matters across cases and what design primitive should guide interpolation or extrapolation. A Type A agent may hand-mint/refine pri_* rows when the operator has asked for a principle or the inspected system supports a complete governed row; single local insights without that authority should route to source note, agent seed, or review first.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/doctrine/principles_curation.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

profile_gapprofile gapnavigationProfile gap marks that an artifact kind has authority or projection data but lacks supported option-surface rowability.

missing row support A profile gap is an explicit navigation debt result record: the kind exists, but the adapter does not yet expose supported flag/card rows or must not claim native bands as implemented support.

ScopeUse profile-gap language when Kind Atlas can see a kind but the option surface cannot honestly browse it yet. The gap prevents legacy commands, native data bands, or generated projections from being mistaken for adapter-supported rowability.

ExampleRepresentative source: system/lib/kind_atlas.py.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

raw_seedraw seedsubstrateSource note is the append-only operator voice system; projections may compress it, but they do not replace it.

operator voice authority layer Source note preserves Will's thinking as source authority. Agents should route through generated indexes, shards, and paper modules before reopening raw markdown, and should never hand-edit generated or raw voice regions.

ScopeUse source note when the task depends on operator intent, voice motifs, reversals, or source provenance. For normal work, use source note query/navigation surfaces and cite par_* refs rather than copying long source text.

ExampleUse this term only where the linked source record uses or governs it.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

reader_packetreader packetartifactA reader packet is a portable guide that helps a human or AI inspect the public source slice without replacing it.

portable inspection guide Reader packet names the generated digest or review artifact that carries routes, summaries, source refs, and boundaries for a public reader. It is a map and handoff, source-linked only.

ScopeUse reader packet for a generated handoff artifact that helps a person or AI inspect the public source slice.

ExampleA reader packet can summarize routes, components, source refs, glossary rows, and scope limits for review.

Not thisDo not treat a reader packet as source authority or as proof that a reviewer read or endorsed it.

Reader ruleUse reader packet when the site gives an AI assistant or reviewer a bounded artifact to start from. The packet should route into source records and keep its scope limits visible.

Also shown asreader digest, review packet

Search handlesAI handoff, copy packet, orientation packet

Related termsroutes_torecordReader packets should route to concrete records.summarizessource_sliceReader packets summarize the public source slice.belowsource_authorityReader packets remain projections below source authority.

rename_migration_receiptrename migration receiptgovernanceA rename migration result record is the governed proof object that the storage_rename_gate in semantic_naming was satisfied; every storage rename must carry one, and blocked renames carry a planned/blocked result record naming the outstanding precondition.

typed storage rename proof Rename migration result records live at state/semantic_naming/rename_receipts/ and follow codex/standards/std_rename_migration_receipt.json. Lifecycle states: planned_gated, planned_blocked, in_progress, completed, reverted. A result record records scope, gate evidence, post-rename obligations, and closure notes so the rename is auditable and reversible.

ScopeUse rename_migration_receipt when you are about to rename a storage path, OR when you are deliberately NOT renaming because a gate precondition fails. The result record is not optional: it is the surface that distinguishes a governed migration from a taste-based rename.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/standards/std_rename_migration_receipt.json.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse rename_migration_receipt when you are about to rename a storage path, OR when you are deliberately NOT renaming because a gate precondition fails. The result record is not optional: it is the surface that distinguishes a governed migration from a taste-based rename.

Also shown asrename receipt, migration receipt

Search handlesstorage rename receipt, rename gate receipt, rename proof, planned rename, planned blocked rename

Related termsspecializessemantic_namingResult records are the migration-gate instrument of the naming grammar.gated_bystandardResult records are validated against std_rename_migration_receipt.json.paired_withrow_jobAudits surface candidates; row jobs and result records govern bounded migration work.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --docs-route "semantic naming option surface"
    Route semantic naming + result record work together.

    Read the governed term definition for result records.

routingroutingnavigationRouting chooses which surface, lane, or next move should answer a situation before source is opened.

choose the right path Routing is the system's path-selection layer. It maps a query, task, path, or state into the smallest useful authority surface, such as docs-route, paper-module lookup, the navigation hologram, source note navigation, or semantic fallback.

ScopeUse routing when the problem is not yet the content itself but the decision of where to look or which lane should own the work. Good routing preserves authority boundaries: it can suggest a paper module, skill, standard, bridge lane, or source note ref, but it does not make the summary authoritative.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/semantic_routing_plane.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse routing when the problem is not yet the content itself but the decision of where to look or which lane should own the work. Good routing preserves authority boundaries: it can suggest a paper module, skill, standard, bridge lane, or source note ref, but it does not make the summary authoritative.

Also shown asroute, route graph, semantic routing

row_jobrow jobruntimeA row job turns broad pressure into one bounded transition against a known row, finding, or artifact slice before any worker or runtime acts.

bounded row transition A row job is a bounded transition against a known row or finding. It declares the target, operation, scope limit, expected patch shape, validation contract, cache key, promotion gate, and result record before Bridge, OpenRouter, NVIDIA, deterministic code, or Type A work is activated.

ScopeUse row_job when a prompt says to use providers, maximize throughput, repair many findings, populate compression bands, or deploy a pipeline. The safe move is to name the target row or finding, operation, worker surface, source fingerprint, validation, and promotion gate first. The row job is a packet grammar, not a live scheduler or authority grant.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md::Row-Job Taxonomy Packet v0.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse row_job when a prompt says to use providers, maximize throughput, repair many findings, populate compression bands, or deploy a pipeline. The safe move is to name the target row or finding, operation, worker surface, source fingerprint, validation, and promotion gate first. The row job is a packet grammar, not a live scheduler or authority grant.

Also shown asrow-job, row_job

rowabilityrowabilitynavigationRowability is the condition where an artifact kind can be browsed, selected, drilled, and verified through stable option-surface rows instead of keyword search.

selectable row readiness Rowability means an artifact kind has a Kind Atlas row, stable ids, adapter-supported cheap bands, source/currentness posture, evidence commands, and omission result records so agents can navigate it without guessing triggers or grepping.

ScopeUse rowability language when deciding whether a repo object is ready for cold-agent browse before retrieval. A rowable kind exposes flag/card option-surface rows, keeps native deeper bands as data unless implemented by the adapter, names gaps honestly, and gives drilldown commands back to source authority.

ExampleRepresentative source: system/lib/standard_option_surface.py.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

A rowable kind exposes flag/card option-surface rows, keeps native deeper bands as data unless implemented by the adapter, names gaps honestly, and gives drilldown commands back to source authority.

Also shown asrowable, rowable control spine

Search handlesrowability plane, rowability before retrieval, selectable row readiness
