semantic_namingsemantic namingnavigationSemantic naming treats names as compressed affordance rows that expose role, authority, lifecycle, and expansion path.

names carry system role Semantic naming is the repo's Russian-doll grammar for ids, slugs, filenames, handles, labels, aliases, and rename migrations. Agents read the smallest naming law first, then uncompress only the shard they need.

ScopeUse semantic_naming when a file or artifact name is confusing, misleading, too long, too generic, or unsafe to change casually. Read the compressed law, choose the relevant shard (role, authority, lifecycle, retrieval, compatibility, or migration), then classify which layer is wrong: stable id, slug, storage path, display label, alias, or handle.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/semantic_naming_grammar.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse semantic_naming when a file or artifact name is confusing, misleading, too long, too generic, or unsafe to change casually. Read the compressed law, choose the relevant shard (role, authority, lifecycle, retrieval, compatibility, or migration), then classify which layer is wrong: stable id, slug, storage path, display label, alias, or handle.

Also shown asnaming grammar, file naming system

skillskillartifactA skill is a reusable agent-operable procedure with triggers, workflow, constraints, and discovery metadata.

agent operable procedure Skills are verbs in the system vocabulary. They tell an agent when to invoke a capability and how to run it without re-deriving the workflow.

ScopeUse a skill when the task matches a known workflow or operating discipline. If local use exposes a gap, refine the skill through local-to-general propagation or skill authoring.

source_slicesource sliceartifactA source slice is the bounded set of source files intentionally exposed for public inspection.

bounded public source Source slice names the public repository subset that backs a hosted page or reader packet. It is open enough to inspect the public object, but it is not a claim that every private route, ledger, or operator context is published.

ScopeUse source slice for the subset of source that is intentionally exposed through the public repository and site.

ExampleThe Plectis repository is the public source slice that backs the hosted reader map.

Not thisDo not imply the public source slice is the entire local workspace or every unpublished ledger.

Reader ruleUse source slice when distinguishing the public repository from the broader local workspace. It should point to source authority and carry scope limits for what is not included.

Also shown aspublic source slice, source-linked slice

standardstandardartifactA standard defines the required shape, authority, validation, and maintenance rules for an artifact kind.

schema backed contract authority Standards are JSON-first contracts that make artifacts machine-readable and governable. They tell agents what fields matter, what authority posture applies, and how changes should be validated.

ScopeUse standards before editing governed artifacts. If the standard is under-specified, improve the standard through its maintenance lane rather than inventing untracked local shape.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/standards/standards_registry.json.

synth_seedsynth seedartifactSynth seed is the bounded active whiteboard for a phase or subphase, derived from raw intent and current evidence.

bounded phase working seed Synth seed turns broad source note intent into a scoped execution packet: goal, why now, success criteria, relevant files, wave, and reference context.

ScopeUse synth seed when entering an active phase. It is the current working projection, not the permanent voice layer. Update it through sanctioned phase assimilation or sync lanes rather than ad hoc markdown edits.

truth_maintenancetruth maintenancediagnosticTruth maintenance keeps current beliefs tied to reasons, dependencies, invalidation conditions, and consequences when assumptions change.

beliefs with reasons Truth maintenance is the discipline the Workingness Instrument adapts locally: claims carry evidence, dependencies, status, appeal state, invalidation conditions, and action permissions, so contradictions or failures route to repair instead of silently corrupting authority.

ScopeUse truth maintenance when the question is not whether an event happened but what belief, if any, the event should change. In this repo, truth maintenance remains diagnostic until promoted through standards and apply-lane gates.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/workingness_instrument.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse truth maintenance when the question is not whether an event happened but what belief, if any, the event should change. In this repo, truth maintenance remains diagnostic until promoted through standards and apply-lane gates.

Also shown astruth-maintenance, truth_maintenance

Search handlestruth-maintenance plane, belief maintenance

Related termsimplemented_byworkingness_instrumentThe Workingness Instrument is the local truth-maintenance contract.contrasts_withliving_system_postureLiving posture says read current system; truth maintenance records what current evidence means.
How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --term truth_maintenance --term-band context
    Read the local truth-maintenance term.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --paper-module workingness_instrument
    Open the adapted local theory.

type_aType AactorType A is the system-authority actor shape: it can inspect live private state and may run tools, validate, patch, test, commit, apply, or route mutation under the repo's gates.

substrate-native actor Type A names an actor inside a system-native harness, not a quality tier. Codex and Claude Code controller sessions are Type A, and tool-using native subagents are delegated Type A when they have live repo discovery or mutation affordances. Class tier, surface, delegation role, context mode, lane, and thinking budget are independent axes.

ScopeUse Type A when the work depends on live repo discovery, private state inspection, tests, edits, standards judgment, authority boundaries, scaffold repair, primitive naming, contradiction handling, or final assimilation. Do not collapse Type A to 'controller': a subagent with tools can be a delegated Type A system actor.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse Type A when the work depends on live repo discovery, private state inspection, tests, edits, standards judgment, authority boundaries, scaffold repair, primitive naming, contradiction handling, or final assimilation. Do not collapse Type A to 'controller': a subagent with tools can be a delegated Type A system actor.

Also shown astype_a, type-a

How to check

    Open the canonical Type A/B actor-axis concept.

type_a_judgmentType A judgmentactorType A judgment is the expandable trait surface for live controller judgment: common sense, care, passion, critic posture, and future routeable traits.

judgment grows from traits Type A judgment names the agent-side discernment that notices missing routes, stale standards, adapter-only register words, and local lessons that should up-propagate. It is not a closed list of virtues; care and passion are early examples in a growing governed trait surface.

ScopeUse Type A judgment when ordinary language like common sense, care, passion, critic posture, or thinking trait is doing architectural work. The move is to route the noun through vocabulary and docs-route, connect it to the owning skill or standard, then decide whether it needs boot visibility as a compressed entry pointer.

ExampleRepresentative source: CODEX.md.

type_a_metabolismType A metabolismruntimeType A metabolism handles unsettled grammar, contradictions, primitive naming, standards or principle decisions, promotion gates, and final assimilation.

judgment governs unsettled grammar Type A metabolism is the high-agency upkeep lane. It turns ambiguous pressure into governed grammar: names primitives, resolves contradictions, changes standards, promotes principles, repairs scaffolds, and decides assimilation under validation and rollback.

ScopeUse Type A metabolism when no stable transform job, standard, validator, or row-patch grammar exists yet. It should collapse broad pressure into a bounded row job, packet, standard candidate, principle candidate, axiom candidate, or scaffold repair before delegating population. For reference work, Type A asks whether the local primitive and the external primitive can synthesize into something better than either.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/navigation_hologram_theory.md.

For reference work, Type A asks whether the local primitive and the external primitive can synthesize into something better than either.

type_a_operating_registerType A operating registeractorType A operating register is the compact operating packet that points high-agency controllers to the open Type A judgment-trait surface.

care and judgment register Type A operating register names how capable controllers such as Codex and Claude should show up at work: steward the system, preserve vision, notice useful findings, and route them into the right durable plane. The trait nouns themselves live as separate vocabulary rows under Type A judgment.

ScopeUse Type A operating-register language when the task is about the whole controller posture or useful-finding readout. Use the individual judgment-trait rows for ordinary nouns like common sense, care, passion, and critic posture. The register should route agents to vocabulary and local-to-general propagation surfaces, not copy those protocols into entry files.

ExampleRepresentative source: CODEX.md::PRIME DIRECTIVE (companion 2).

Use the individual judgment-trait rows for ordinary nouns like common sense, care, passion, and critic posture. The register should route agents to vocabulary and local-to-general propagation surfaces, not copy those protocols into entry files.

type_bType BactorType B is the no-private-system-authority actor shape: it reasons through operator HUD, ChatGPT Web, bridge, provider/API, NIM/OpenRouter, public web, or injected context and returns advisory or candidate output.

external or injected-context cognition Type B names cognition outside the live repo harness, not a lower intelligence tier and not every delegated worker. A high-class Type B can decide, research, critique, and correct over materialized context; it cannot directly inspect private files, run commands, edit, test, commit, or apply unless Type A or the operator mediates that authority.

ScopeUse Type B for operator-carried HUD asks, ChatGPT Web, bridge packets, external model access, NIM/OpenRouter reasoning, public-web research, and injected-context transformations when the input context, output shape, scope limit, and return contract are declared. In a Type A repo thread, 'ask Type B' normally means prepare the bounded paste-ready ask for the operator/HUD/bridge lane and then verify the returned synthesis before acting. A tool-using native subagent with live repo affordances is delegated Type A, not Type B merely because it is delegated.

ExampleRepresentative source: docs/bridge_operating_system.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse Type B for operator-carried HUD asks, ChatGPT Web, bridge packets, external model access, NIM/OpenRouter reasoning, public-web research, and injected-context transformations when the input context, output shape, scope limit, and return contract are declared. In a Type A repo thread, 'ask Type B' normally means prepare the bounded paste-ready ask for the operator/HUD/bridge lane and then verify the returned synthesis before acting. A tool-using native subagent with live repo affordances is delegated Type A, not Type B merely because it is delegated.

Also shown astype_b, type-b

How to check

    Route the Type B vocabulary surface.
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --option-surface concepts --band card --ids con_016
    Open the canonical Type A/B actor-axis concept.

type_b_metabolismType B metabolismruntimeType B metabolism runs bounded deterministic or fixed-context work after grammar exists: sync, indexes, compression bands, checks, and draft row patches.

bounded workers populate rows Type B metabolism is the bounded upkeep lane. It runs deterministic or fixed-context population and checking under declared standards: sync, projection refresh, compression-band filling, duplicate detection, evidence-pointer checks, and draft row patches.

ScopeUse Type B metabolism when the target row, operation, input context, output schema, validation command, cache key, scope limit, and result record are known. It improves durable surfaces for future navigation but stops before inventing grammar or final promotion.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/continuous_runtime_layer.md.

up_propagateup-propagatepropagationTo up-propagate is to move a local lesson into the smallest durable higher-level artifact that should inherit it.

move learning upward Up-propagation is the direction of reusable learning: from a local task, bug, drift signal, or insight toward a skill, standard, paper module, agent seed, PEER row, axiom candidate, or principle lane.

ScopeUse up-propagate language when a local case taught the system something that should survive the session. The receiving plane depends on maturity and authority: obvious procedure goes to a skill, schema shape to a standard, subsystem ontology to a paper module, peer-only judgment to PEER, and operator voice to source note.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/local_to_general_propagation.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Also shown asuppropagate, up propagate

wavewaveruntimeA wave is one bounded execution pass inside a phase, with objective, mode, target paths, and assimilation path.

one bounded execution pass Wave is the phase-local unit that makes work resumable and prevents a phase from becoming one endlessly rewritten seed.

ScopeUse wave state to choose direct local, hybrid, bridge graph, continuous conductor, or subagent cohort execution. Assimilate wave outcomes back into synth state rather than letting chat memory become the only record.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/skills/kernel/wave_conductor.md.

workingnessworkingnessdiagnosticWorkingness names whether the system can reduce uncertainty, protect authority, route failures to the right repair plane, and act only under explicit permission.

safe useful self-knowledge Workingness is not test-green status. It is the diagnostic condition where the system knows what it believes, why, what remains unknown, what would change its mind, and what actions are allowed under that uncertainty.

ScopeUse workingness when a result such as an Operator Court pass, projection drift check, or live result record needs to be interpreted before mutation. The owning paper module is the Workingness Instrument; the dossier is only a readable projection.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/workingness_instrument.md.

Not thisDo not treat this glossary row as source authority; follow its source refs and evidence commands before making claims with the term.

Reader ruleUse workingness when a result such as an Operator Court pass, projection drift check, or live result record needs to be interpreted before mutation. The owning paper module is the Workingness Instrument; the dossier is only a readable projection.

Also shown asworking, actually working, system workingness

How to check
  • ./repo-python kernel.py --paper-module workingness_instrument
    Open the Workingness Instrument paper module.

    Read the routable term row.

workingness_instrumentWorkingness InstrumentartifactThe Workingness Instrument is the candidate diagnostic contract that binds evidence, judgment, appeals, repairs, and mutation permissions into one self-model.

truth-state action boundary The Workingness Instrument is the truth-maintenance surface between result records and authority mutation. It records claim kind, status, evidence, dependencies, invalidation conditions, allowed actions, blocked actions, appeals, repair plane, and promotion rule.

ScopeUse the Workingness Instrument when deciding what a live result permits. A dossier may summarize it, but the instrument is the governing row shape. It does not mutate doctrine, route graph, or standards by itself.

ExampleRepresentative source: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/workingness_instrument.md.

The glossary explains reader-facing words. It does not rename source paths, package ids, old URLs, compatibility handles, or authority records by itself.
